Qwen2ForCausalLM: A family of decoder-only Transformer-based large language models developed by Alibaba Cloud. The Qwen2 series is trained with large-scale multilingual corpora using the next-token prediction objective, and includes both base and instruction-tuned variants across multiple parameter scales ranging from hundreds of millions to tens of billions of parameters. The models incorporate improvements in training data quality, alignment techniques, and long-context handling, enabling strong performance in natural language understanding, generation, reasoning, coding, and multilingual tasks.
Gemma2ForCausalLM: A family of open large language models released by Google, built on a decoder-only Transformer architecture and optimized for efficient inference and high-quality language modeling. Gemma 2 introduces architectural refinements such as grouped-query attention and interleaved local–global attention mechanisms to improve memory efficiency and long-context processing. The family provides pretrained and instruction-tuned models at multiple scales (e.g., 2B, 9B, and 27B parameters) and is designed to support a wide range of natural language understanding and generation applications.
LlamaForCausalLM: A family of autoregressive large language models developed by Meta AI and based on the decoder-only Transformer architecture. The LLaMA series is designed with a strong emphasis on training efficiency and high performance relative to model size, leveraging large-scale curated text corpora and optimized training strategies. The models serve as widely adopted foundation models for research and downstream applications, including instruction-following systems, conversational agents, and domain-specific adaptations through fine-tuning.
MistralForCausalLM: A family of high-efficiency decoder-only Transformer language models developed by Mistral AI. The architecture incorporates techniques such as grouped-query attention and sliding-window attention to improve computational efficiency, memory usage, and scalability for long-context inference. Mistral models are designed to deliver strong language understanding and generation capabilities while maintaining a favorable performance-to-parameter ratio, making them suitable for both research and production deployment.
MixtralForCausalLM: A family of mixture-of-experts (MoE) decoder-only Transformer language models developed by Mistral AI. Mixtral extends the Mistral architecture by incorporating a sparse mixture-of-experts mechanism in which multiple expert feed-forward networks are selectively activated for each token through a routing strategy. This design significantly increases model capacity while maintaining efficient inference by activating only a subset of experts during computation. Mixtral models leverage techniques such as grouped-query attention and sliding-window attention, enabling strong performance in natural language understanding, generation, reasoning, and coding tasks, while achieving a favorable balance between scalability, efficiency, and performance.
